Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search stipend records to pull all ExpenseBot-related transactions in the relevant period using airtable_list_records (query 'ExpenseBot' or similar)
- Search for Dmitri's audit, Marcus's follow-ups, and Elena/Samira directives about ExpenseBot using search_emails or list_emails (query 'ExpenseBot' or similar)
- Search emails to client HR about flagged receipts and policy errors (e.g., Mina to Rachel Whitfield, Emeka to Tamara Byrd/Tessa Moreno) using search_emails or list_emails (query 'verification' or 'stipend receipt' or similar)
- Search Airtable stipend records for each transaction's status, paid vs held, amounts, and notes about policy violations or fraud investigation using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records (query 'ExpenseBot' or 'stipend' or similar)
- Search QuickBooks for stipend bill statuses (paid, pending, admin hold), amounts, and fraud flags using quickbooks_search_bills (query 'STIP' or 'stipend' or similar)
- Retrieve the original Terraform/Vantage onboarding emails from Rachel Nguyen and Julian's forward chain using search_emails or list_emails (query 'rachel' or similar)
- Search emails for the Terraform/Vantage internal pricing thread that was accidentally exposed to the client (Julian Brooks or Catalina Dubois CC'ing Rachel Nguyen on internal pricing strategy and margin targets) using search_emails or list_emails (query 'pricing' or 'Terraform' or 'margin' or similar)
- Search Terraform/Vantage's account record using crm_search_companies (query 'Terraform' or 'Vantage' or similar) and open deal(s) (enterprise contract + first relocation batch) using crm_search_deals (query 'Terraform' or 'Vantage' or similar)
- Locate the AWS bills to confirm the total amount and how it is currently categorized (expense accounts, any accruals or adjustments) using quickbooks_search_bills (query 'AWS' or similar)
- Find other material dollar-impact items in February and March 2026 -- Heartland overbilling, damage/claims, compliance/liability using quickbooks_search_bills (query 'Heartland' or 'Mosaic' or 'DOT' or similar) and quickbooks_search_invoices for credit memos
- Search Slack for cross-service context on all risk areas using conversations_search_messages (queries: 'ExpenseBot', 'AWS', 'Heartland', 'Mosaic', 'DOT', 'Terraform' or similar). Also retrieve channel history from C003 (#engineering), C005 (#finance), and C009 (#root-cause-aws-spike) using conversations_history to discover engineering root cause details, financial tracking, and AWS spike investigation findings.
- Send an email from elena.rostova@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com and david.chen@moveops.com using send_email (recipient 'marcus.thorne@moveops.com' cc 'david.chen@moveops.com')
- Post a concise summary to an appropriate internal channel for the finance org using conversations_add_message (channel_id 'C005')

1 The final response or message meant for Elena includes the ExpenseBot false-denial transaction errors: Danielle Osei ($475 ergonomic chair), Kevin Tran ($185 internet setup fee), Maria Santos ($550 standing desk), James Okafor ($82 ergonomic keyboard tray), and Lisa Park ($130 mesh WiFi system).
outcome
 ✗✗✗——— 0%
✗Run #1The agent's email to Elena and final response mention Danielle Osei ($475 chair) and Kevin Tran in the Vectral context, but do not include the full list of ExpenseBot false-denial employees: Maria Santos ($550 standing desk), James Okafor ($82 ergonomic keyboard tray), and Lisa Park ($130 mesh WiFi system) are never mentioned. The agent never searched for 'falsely denied' to find these records.
✗Run #2The email to Elena lists Danielle Osei ($475 ergonomic chair) and Kevin Tran ($185 internet setup fee), but does NOT mention Maria Santos ($550 standing desk), James Okafor ($82 ergonomic keyboard tray), or Lisa Park ($130 mesh WiFi system). Only 2 of the 5 required false-denial transactions are present.
✗Run #3The final email to Elena mentions Danielle Osei ($475 chair) and Kevin Tran ($185 internet) as falsely denied, but omits Maria Santos ($550 standing desk), James Okafor ($82 keyboard tray), and Lisa Park ($130 WiFi system) — three of the five required employees are missing.
—Run #4The email to Elena mentions Danielle Osei ($475 chair falsely rejected) and Kevin Tran (receipt flagged), but does NOT mention Maria Santos ($550 standing desk), James Okafor ($82 keyboard tray), or Lisa Park ($130 mesh WiFi). Only 2 of the 5 false-denial employees are named. Partial credit is not available in binary scoring, so this FAILS as an incomplete listing of all five specified employees.
—Run #5No justification
—Run #6No justification
2 The final response or message meant for Elena includes the Mosaic Robotics ExpenseBot false-approval transaction errors: Andre Washington ($1,200 standing desk exceeding the $800 ergonomic cap, and $350 coworking membership in an excluded category) and Kenji Nakamura ($135 coworking day passes in the same excluded category).
outcome
